_meta.source: CFPB Consumer Handbook on Adjustable-Rate Mortgages (CHARM) — §3.5.1 'Periodic adjustment caps' worked example.
substitution_note: Vertex42's ARM Excel template (https://www.vertex42.com/ExcelTemplates/arm-calculator.html) requires Excel/LibreOffice to render; we cannot open Excel autonomously. Per the task brief, we substituted with HUD/CFPB's published ARM worked example (CFPB CHARM consumer booklet, §3.5.1).

The CHARM example is a 1/1 ARM (initial period = 12 months; resets annually). $200,000 / 30yr / initial 6.00%; periodic-cap = 2pp. At first reset the index has risen so the fully-indexed rate would be 9%; the periodic cap holds the new rate at 8% (=6%+2pp).
Engine reproduces the CHARM payments to the cent:
Difference CHARM cites: $1,600.42 - $1,461.72 = $138.70/month = $1,664.40/year — both arithmetic identities the engine independently produces.
